ko: 교훈 4. 로봇을 일하러 보낼 수 있습니다. 토바가 집에 있는데 카페를 연 친구를 만났어요

en: Or this could be like when you go to the supermarket and they say no pets are allowed in the icon
ko: 농담으로 토바를 가게에 데려와서 도와주게 하자고 했어요

en: in the window and be decided upon, like a policy, each building or business.
ko: 배웠어요

en: Or this could look like government-issue ID cards for robots with their individually given permissions.
ko: AI와 로봇에 의한 노동 대체 논의는

en: This is, like, where they can go, what they can do, approved and managed by a centralized
ko: AI와 로봇에 의한 노동 대체 논의는

en: regulation office with, like, trainings and licensing and things like this.
ko: 항상 재앙적 발언을 끌어당기죠

en: It's completely up to us to decide. Lesson four is that you can send your robot to work.
So Tova was at home, and I went out with a friend who had just opened up a local cafe.
ko: 하지만 더 실용적인 대화도 해야 합니다

en: And jokingly, he was like, oh, let's bring Tova to help out around the store, this is such a good idea.
ko: 공정한 세금 체계는 어떤 모습일까요? 로봇 노동자 도입의

en: We had a laugh about it, but it was, like, playing over in my mind, in my mind.
ko: 공정한 세금 체계는 어떤 모습일까요? 로봇 노동자 도입의

en: And then we brought it over and it learned how to help out at a store.
ko: 현재 사회 시스템에 넣을지 보편적 기본소득을 개발할지

en: Now this discussion of labor displacement by AI and robotics, it's one of those discussions
ko: 이런 공포를 조장하는 발언은 재정 시스템을 보존하면서

en: that just attracts those large catastrophic statements.
ko: AI의 효율성을 수용하는 방법에 대한 실용적 논의에 자리를 내줘야 합니다

en: I'm sure you've all heard them. Robots are taking our jobs, no one's going to have a job in the future.
ko: 교훈 5. 로봇은 우리의 최선과 최악을 끌어냅니다

en: These are obviously such interesting, fascinating, and terrifying conversations.
ko: 개가 있다고 했죠. 매일 아침 광장에서 산책하면서 로봇을 가져가요

en: The conversations that I would love us to be also having alongside that are of a more
ko: 반응이 정말 다양해요. 어느 날 한 엄마가

en: practical nature, right? What does a fair tax system look like?
ko: 로봇에서 최대한 멀리 서 있으면서 아이들에게 가서 말 걸어봐라고 하더라고요

en: To facilitate the advantages for business owners of bringing in robotic workers, of
ko: 로봇에서 최대한 멀리 서 있으면서 아이들에게 가서 말 걸어봐라고 하더라고요

en: automating using AI to increase efficiency, while still contributing to the public funds
ko: 아이들이 정말 좋은 질문을 했어요. 로봇 일을 하려면 뭘 공부해야 해요?

en: that support our communities, right?
ko: 아이들이 정말 좋은 질문을 했어요. 로봇 일을 하려면 뭘 공부해야 해요?

en: Are we taxing just the sale of the robot, or are we taxing the value that it adds?
ko: 로봇이 손 흔들어줄 수 있어요? 달리기 시합하자. 다음 날에는

en: Are we going to put those funds into our current social systems, or are we going to scrap it
ko: 노신사 한 분이 토바의 팔을 부러뜨리려 했어요

en: and try to develop universal basic income system instead?
ko: 몸에서 뜯어내려고

en: These broad, fear-mongering statements, they have to give way to practical discussions
ko: 몸에서 뜯어내려고

en: about how to preserve our financial systems, while still allowing us to embrace the efficiencies
ko: 뭐 하시는 거예요 했더니 옛날 방식의 악수라고

en: offered by AI.
ko: 그래, 그렇게 보였나 봐. 요점은 사람들의

en: Lesson five is that robots really bring out the best in us and the worst in us.
ko: 반응이 정말 다양하다는 거예요. 나이, 성별, 문화에 따라

en: So I mentioned I have a dog, I walk my dog in the square every morning, and I bring my
ko: 그리고 흥미롭게도 미디어 선입견이 있어요

en: And the reactions that I get, they run the gamut.
ko: 아침에 스크롤하며 본 것부터 로봇과의 첫 경험까지. 스타트렉의 데이터든

en: So, one day, I had a mom, and she was standing pretty much as far back as a human could stand
ko: 아침에 스크롤하며 본 것부터 로봇과의 첫 경험까지. 스타트렉의 데이터든

en: from the robot, but encouraging her kids, go, talk, talk, and they were asking such good questions.
ko: 이런 역사를 가지고 있고 실제 로봇 앞에 서면

en: What should I study at school if I want to work with a robot?
ko: 이런 역사를 가지고 있고 실제 로봇 앞에 서면

en: Can you make the robot wave at me? Can I race the robot?
ko: 실망할 수 있어요

en: In the square, very sweet.
ko: 실망할 수 있어요

en: In addition, the following day, I had an older gentleman walk up and try to break Tova's
ko: 실망할 수 있어요

en: arm right off of its body, exactly.
ko: 현재 수준을 보여주고 정확한 이해를 제공해서 현실에 맞는 규제와 논의를

en: When I said to him, what are you doing, he said, oh, a good old-fashioned firm handshake,
ko: 할 수 있습니다

en: for sure. Yeah, okay. It looked like that.
ko: 실용성의 보이지 않는 손은 미래로 나아가면서 역사의 궤적을 계속 형성할 겁니다

en: So, the bottom line is that people, they have these very, very wide reactions, right?
ko: 실용성의 보이지 않는 손은 미래로 나아가면서 역사의 궤적을 계속 형성할 겁니다

en: And this can be due to any number of factors.
ko: 두려움이나 흥분의 선정주의에 빠지기보다

en: We have age, gender, culture, and, interestingly enough, media preconceptions.
ko: 이 논의를 현실로 가져와서 체화된 AI와의 삶의 실용적 본질을

en: This is like everything from what they were scrolling on that morning and seeing a brand
ko: 받아들이고 함께 미래를 규제할 준비를 하자고 부탁합니다

en: new video to their first introduction to robotics, right, if that was Mr. Data on Star
ko: 받아들이고 함께 미래를 규제할 준비를 하자고 부탁합니다

en: Trek or C-3PO in Star Wars or Rosie on the Jetsons.
ko: 다음에 누군가 로봇 종말에 대해 이야기하면 이렇게 말하세요

en: Everybody comes with this, like, really, really sort of a whole history behind them, and when
ko: 좋아 좋아 근데 로봇도 트램 표가 필요하다고 생각해?

en: they finally get in front of a robot, it can be so disappointing.
ko: 감사합니다

en: So disappointing. I think we need to have a lot more interaction between the public and robotics so that we
can actually show people where we're at and give a more accurate understanding so that
we can regulate and have discussions for reality in a more practical format.
The invisible hand of practicality will continue to shape the trajectory of history as we move
into the future.
So, instead of being wrapped up in this sensationalism of fear or excitement, I ask that we bring
these discussions down into reality, that we embrace the practical nature of life alongside
embodied AI as we prepare to regulate the future together.
So next time that somebody tries to talk to you about the robot apocalypse, I want you
to turn to them and say, okay, okay, okay, but do you think that robots need a ticket on the tram?
Thank you. Thank you. Thank you.
ko: 감사합니다, 감사합니다
